situation: Bottom player has closed guard locked with overhooks or wrist control, top player is posturing inside the guard.
topObjective: Maintain strong posture, keep elbows tight, and work to open the closed guard without getting swept or submitted.
bottomObjective: Break posture, control a wrist, cut an angle, and finish the triangle choke.
coachCue: Cut the angle — your hips must be off-center before you lock the triangle.
scoring: 2pt per triangle locked, 3pt per tap, 1pt per sweep to mounted triangle

situation: Bottom player has closed guard with a two-on-one grip on the top player's wrist, top player is trying to posture and free the arm.
topObjective: Defend the kimura by keeping your elbow glued to your body and posturing up; work to pass the guard.
bottomObjective: Isolate the arm, secure the figure-four grip, hip out, and finish the kimura.
coachCue: Pin the wrist to the mat first — never chase the kimura grip while they're still postured.
scoring: 2pt per kimura grip secured, 3pt per tap, 1pt for successfully defending and passing

situation: Bottom player has closed guard, top player is working to open and pass; live sparring from closed guard.
topObjective: Open the closed guard and establish a passing position without getting swept or submitted.
bottomObjective: Sweep the top player to achieve a dominant top position or submit from guard.
coachCue: Bottom player: always re-break posture before attacking — no posture break, no sweep.
scoring: 1pt per sweep, 2pt per guard pass, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player has established full mount with hands on the mat for base, bottom player has arms defensively framed.
topObjective: Maintain mount and hunt for submissions; re-mount if displaced.
bottomObjective: Trap an arm and a foot on the same side, bridge explosively, and escape to guard or top position.
coachCue: Bridge into them, not up — drive your hips toward the trapped side at a 45-degree angle.
scoring: 2pt per successful upa escape, 1pt per remount, 3pt per submission from mount

situation: Top player has established mount in a competition-pace scenario; bottom player is actively defending and trying to escape.
topObjective: Maintain mount pressure, transition between low mount and high mount, and finish a submission under competition intensity.
bottomObjective: Escape mount using any legal technique and recover to a neutral or dominant position.
coachCue: Top player: swim your hands inside their frames — whoever wins the underhook battle controls the mount.
scoring: ADCC scoring: 2pt mount, 1pt escape to guard, sub wins the round

situation: Attacker has seatbelt grip with hooks in from back control; defender has chin tucked and hands fighting the choking arm.
topObjective: Maintain back control, keep hooks active, and attack the rear naked choke.
bottomObjective: Clear the hooks, turn into the attacker, fight the choking hand to the low side, and escape to guard or top.
coachCue: Get to the side of the underhook — escape toward the weak side of the seatbelt, never toward the choking arm.
scoring: 2pt per clean escape to guard, 3pt per submission, 1pt per hook cleared

situation: Attacker has a dominant position (side control or turtle) and is hunting to take the back; defender is protecting their neck and hips.
topObjective: Secure a seatbelt grip, insert hooks or body triangle, and establish full back control.
bottomObjective: Deny the seatbelt, block hook insertion, and return to a neutral guard position.
coachCue: Seatbelt first, then the first hook — never jump to the back without upper body control.
scoring: 3pt per back take with both hooks, 2pt per seatbelt with one hook, 1pt per escape to guard

situation: Top player has established side control with crossface and underhook, bottom player has frames on the hip and neck.
topObjective: Maintain side control, neutralize frames, and advance to mount or north-south.
bottomObjective: Create enough space with frames and hip escapes to recover guard or get to knees.
coachCue: Frame on the neck and hip, shrimp to create space — never flat on your back, always on your side.
scoring: 2pt per guard recovery, 2pt per mount transition, 3pt per submission

situation: Bottom player has half guard with a knee shield and underhook battle is active; top player is working to flatten and pass.
topObjective: Kill the knee shield, establish crossface, flatten the bottom player, and work to pass half guard.
bottomObjective: Maintain the knee shield, fight for the underhook, and recover to full guard or sweep.
coachCue: Never lose the knee shield and the underhook at the same time — if one goes, fight twice as hard for the other.
scoring: 1pt per guard recovery, 2pt per half guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 3pt per submission

situation: Competition-intensity half guard battle; bottom player pulling half guard, top player engaging immediately to pass.
topObjective: Pass the half guard using any technique within competition rules and advance to a scoring position.
bottomObjective: Sweep or submit from half guard under competition pressure; recover full guard if losing position.
coachCue: Underhook wins half guard — whoever gets it first dictates the exchange.
scoring: ADCC scoring: 3pt guard pass, 2pt sweep, sub wins round

situation: Top player has nearly passed half guard; bottom player has only a quarter guard (ankle or foot trapped between top player's legs).
topObjective: Extract your trapped foot, complete the pass, and establish a dominant position.
bottomObjective: Use the quarter guard to re-enter half guard, recover full guard, or find an underhook to sweep.
coachCue: Bottom: get on your side immediately — if you stay flat in quarter guard you will get passed.
scoring: 2pt per full guard recovery from quarter guard, 3pt per completed pass, 1pt per half guard recovery

situation: Attacker has outside ashi garami with heel exposure; defender is working to free the knee line and disengage.
topObjective: Control the heel, break the knee line, apply rotational force, and finish the outside heel hook.
bottomObjective: Clear the knee line, turn into the attacker to relieve rotational pressure, and extract your leg.
coachCue: Control the toes with your wrist, not your fingers — rotate the heel by turning your whole body.
scoring: 3pt per heel hook finish, 2pt per heel exposure with control, 1pt per successful leg extraction

situation: Attacker has an ashi garami position and is hunting for a heel hook; defender must escape before the submission is locked.
topObjective: Maintain leg entanglement, expose the heel, and attack the heel hook.
bottomObjective: Boot your foot free, clear the knee line, and disengage safely to a neutral position.
coachCue: Turn your knee toward the attacker and push the hips — never pull straight back, that exposes the heel.
scoring: 2pt per clean leg escape, 3pt per heel hook, 1pt per re-guard after leg escape

situation: Both players are seated; either can engage the legs to initiate a leg lock exchange or pull guard.
topObjective: Enter into a dominant leg entanglement (inside sankaku, outside ashi, or 50/50), expose the heel, and finish.
bottomObjective: Win the leg entanglement battle, achieve a superior ashi position, and attack or sweep.
coachCue: Win the inside position — whoever controls the inside space controls the leg lock game.
scoring: 1pt per entry to ashi garami, 2pt per heel exposure, 3pt per submission

situation: Attacker has inside sankaku (411/honey hole) with one heel hook attempt defended; chaining to the next attack.
topObjective: Chain heel hook attempts by transitioning between inside sankaku, outside ashi, and 50/50 as the defender escapes each position.
bottomObjective: Defend each heel hook attempt, clear your legs, and work to disengage or reverse the entanglement.
coachCue: When they clear the first hook, immediately backstep to outside ashi — never let them fully escape between attacks.
scoring: 1pt per successful transition to new entanglement, 3pt per submission, 2pt per complete escape to neutral

situation: Competition-pace leg lock scenario starting from a seated guard pull; both players engage for leg entanglements immediately.
topObjective: Win the leg entanglement exchange, achieve a dominant ashi position, and finish a heel hook under competition pressure.
bottomObjective: Counter-attack the leg locks, reverse to a dominant leg position, or disengage and come on top.
coachCue: First to the inside heel wins — speed of entry matters more than grip strength in competition leg locks.
duration: 5-min rounds, EBI overtime rules for draws
scoring: Sub wins round; 2pt per dominant leg control position, 1pt per escape

situation: Top player is standing, bottom player has open guard with feet on hips; top player is gripping both legs hunting for the leg drag.
topObjective: Execute the leg drag by pulling one leg across the body, pinning the hip, and completing the pass to side control.
bottomObjective: Prevent the leg drag by framing on the knee, recovering hip position, and re-establishing open guard.
coachCue: Staple the hip to the mat after the drag — if their hip is free, they will re-guard.
scoring: 3pt per completed leg drag pass, 1pt per guard recovery, 2pt per sweep from guard

situation: Top player is in the bottom player's open guard, working to establish chest-to-chest pressure and eliminate space.
topObjective: Close the distance, establish heavy chest pressure, kill the legs, and methodically pass to side control.
bottomObjective: Maintain frames, create distance, use guard retention to prevent the pressure pass, and look for sweeps.
coachCue: Head position wins pressure passing — drive your head under their chin and make them carry your weight.
scoring: 3pt per guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per guard recovery after near-pass

situation: Top player is in half guard working to smash pass; bottom player has a knee shield but top player is pressuring through it.
topObjective: Drive through the knee shield with shoulder pressure, flatten the bottom player, and complete the smash pass.
bottomObjective: Use the knee shield to maintain distance, recover full guard, or sweep when the top player over-commits.
coachCue: Top: drop your hips and drive your shoulder into their jaw line — heavy hips kill the knee shield.
scoring: 3pt per completed smash pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per full guard recovery

situation: Bottom player has open guard (butterfly, seated, or feet-on-hips); top player is standing and engaging to pass.
topObjective: Shut down the open guard, strip the hooks and frames, and pass to a dominant position.
bottomObjective: Use open guard hooks and grips to off-balance the top player and execute a sweep to top position.
coachCue: Bottom: your head must be higher than your hips at the moment of the sweep — sit up into them.
scoring: 2pt per sweep to top, 3pt per guard pass, 1pt per guard recovery

situation: Bottom player is turtled with elbows and knees tight; top player is on top working to break the turtle down.
topObjective: Break the turtle by inserting hooks, securing a seatbelt, and transitioning to back control or a front headlock.
bottomObjective: Protect the neck and hips, deny hooks, and either sit out to guard or stand up to base.
coachCue: Top: get chest-to-back contact first — you cannot insert hooks from a distance.
scoring: 3pt per back take, 2pt per front headlock with control, 1pt per turtle escape to guard, 2pt per stand-up escape

situation: Competition-pace standing or clinch exchange where one player is hunting the guillotine off a failed shot or in the scramble.
topObjective: Set up and finish the guillotine choke from any position — standing, seated, or in guard — under competition pressure.
bottomObjective: Defend the guillotine by posturing, circling to the safe side, and clearing the neck grip to escape.
coachCue: Chin strap grip and pull the elbow to your chest — power comes from arching your back, not squeezing your arm.
scoring: 3pt per guillotine finish, 2pt per locked guillotine with guard, 1pt per successful defense and pass

situation: Competition-pace scramble where one player has a front headlock and is looking to lock up the darce or anaconda choke.
topObjective: Secure the darce or anaconda grip from the front headlock, lock the figure-four, and finish the choke.
bottomObjective: Defend the choke by framing, keeping your trapped arm tight, and working to clear the head and escape.
coachCue: Walk your hips to the choking side — the darce finishes when your hip pressure drives their shoulder into their neck.
scoring: 3pt per darce/anaconda finish, 2pt per locked choke position, 1pt per escape to neutral

situation: Competition-pace guard scenario starting from closed guard; attacker is specifically hunting the triangle choke with full resistance.
topObjective: Defend the triangle by maintaining posture, keeping both arms in or both arms out, and work to pass.
bottomObjective: Set up and finish the triangle choke using competition-level setups including hip bump to triangle and overhook triangle.
coachCue: Squeeze your knees together before you pull the head — the choke comes from the legs, the hand just finishes it.
scoring: 3pt per triangle finish, 2pt per locked triangle, 1pt per posture recovery and near-pass

situation: Film study scenario — players recreate positions from recent ADCC matches and drill the exchanges live.
topObjective: Execute the top player's strategy from the studied match — apply the same passing, pressure, and submission sequences.
bottomObjective: Execute the bottom player's strategy from the studied match — apply the same guard, sweeps, and submissions.
coachCue: Focus on the transitions between positions, not just the final technique — ADCC matches are won in the micro-adjustments.
scoring: ADCC scoring: 3pt pass, 2pt sweep, 2pt takedown, sub wins round

situation: Both players are seated or in open guard, engaging in a live leg lock exchange mimicking competition scrambles.
topObjective: Win the leg entanglement, achieve inside position, expose the heel, and finish the heel hook.
bottomObjective: Counter the leg entries, fight for inside position, and either submit or disengage to come on top.
coachCue: Track the knee line — whoever controls the knee line controls the entire exchange.
scoring: 3pt per submission, 2pt per dominant entanglement with heel exposure, 1pt per escape to top

situation: Top player is caught in a triangle from closed guard; the triangle is partially locked but not yet fully cinched.
topObjective: Defend and escape the triangle by posturing, stacking, and working your trapped arm across to relieve the choke.
bottomObjective: Finish the triangle by cutting the angle, pulling the head down, and squeezing to complete the choke.
coachCue: Posture immediately and stack — the longer you stay bent over in the triangle, the tighter it gets.
duration: 2-min rounds, start in the triangle position
scoring: 3pt per triangle escape to pass, 3pt per triangle finish, 1pt per re-lock after escape attempt

situation: Both players are in a neutral clinch position or one player is inside the other's open guard; focus is on establishing and maintaining correct posture.
topObjective: Maintain upright posture with a straight spine, keep elbows connected to your body, and resist all attempts to break your posture.
bottomObjective: Use grips and pulls to break the top player's posture down, collapsing their spine and bringing their head toward the mat.
coachCue: Eyes up, chest up, hips forward — if your head drops, everything else follows.
scoring: 1pt per 5-second posture maintenance under pressure, 1pt per posture break held for 3 seconds

situation: Both players start on their knees; one player pushes, pulls, and tries to off-balance the other while both work to maintain base.
topObjective: Maintain a wide, stable base while your partner tries to push, pull, and tip you over from all angles.
bottomObjective: Off-balance the other player using push-pull combinations and circular movement to break their base.
coachCue: Three points of contact at all times — widen your knees, post a hand when you feel the pull.
scoring: 1pt per successful off-balance (hand or knee touches mat), 1pt per 10 seconds of base maintained under pressure

situation: Both players are standing or in an open guard exchange; the focus is on hand fighting for wrist control, collar ties, and underhooks without gi grips.
topObjective: Win dominant grips — collar tie, wrist control, or underhook — and use them to set up a pass or takedown entry.
bottomObjective: Strip the opponent's grips, establish your own dominant grips, and use them to off-balance or set up attacks.
coachCue: Never let them have a grip for free — if they grab you, strip it within two seconds or use it against them.
duration: 3-min rounds, grip fighting only (no takedowns or passes)
scoring: 1pt per dominant grip held for 3 seconds, 2pt per double underhook control, 1pt per grip strip

situation: Bottom player has closed guard with a controlled wrist and is working to set up the triangle; top player is defending with posture.
topObjective: Maintain posture, keep both arms in or both arms out of the guard, and prevent the angle needed for the triangle.
bottomObjective: Break posture, isolate one arm across the centerline using a push-pull combination, and shoot the legs up to lock the triangle.
coachCue: Push the arm across your body, then immediately hip out and shoot your leg over the shoulder — timing beats strength.
scoring: 2pt per triangle locked, 3pt per tap, 1pt per posture recovery that prevents the setup

situation: Bottom player has closed guard with an overhook controlling one arm; top player is defending with elbow tight and posturing.
topObjective: Free the trapped arm, maintain posture, and work to open the closed guard for a pass attempt.
bottomObjective: Control the arm with the overhook, hip out, isolate the arm, and attack the armbar with correct grip and hip placement.
coachCue: Pinch your knees tight and elevate your hips — the armbar finishes with hip pressure, not arm pulling.
scoring: 2pt per armbar grip secured with hips elevated, 3pt per tap, 1pt per arm extraction

situation: Both players are in an open guard exchange; attacker is looking to enter into a leg entanglement from standing or seated guard.
topObjective: Enter into ashi garami, outside ashi, or inside sankaku from a guard pass attempt, a sweep defense, or a direct entry.
bottomObjective: Deny the leg entries by keeping knees tight, fighting the initial bite, and clearing the legs before entanglement locks in.
coachCue: Control the ankle first, then enter — a heel hook entry without ankle control is just a scramble.
scoring: 2pt per clean entry to any ashi position, 1pt per entry denied and guard recovered, 3pt per submission

situation: Attacker has ashi garami locked; focus is on understanding the mechanics of the position — hip alignment, knee line, and leverage angles.
topObjective: Demonstrate and apply correct ashi garami mechanics — proper hip placement, knee line control, and shoulder-to-heel connection.
bottomObjective: Identify and exploit gaps in the attacker's ashi mechanics to escape — misaligned hips, loose knee line, or free heel.
coachCue: Your hips and their heel must be on the same line — if your hips are offline, the heel hook has no power.
scoring: 2pt per mechanically correct heel hook position held for 5 seconds, 1pt per mechanical flaw identified and exploited

situation: Defender is turtled with tight elbows and knees; attacker has chest-to-back contact and is hunting submissions from the turtle.
topObjective: Attack the turtle with chokes (short choke, clock choke equivalent without gi), arm-in guillotine, or crucifix entries.
bottomObjective: Defend submissions by keeping elbows tight, tucking the chin, and working to sit out, granby roll, or stand up.
coachCue: Top: if the neck is hidden, attack the far arm for the crucifix — they can't protect both forever.
scoring: 3pt per submission from turtle, 2pt per back take, 2pt per turtle escape to guard or standing

situation: Top player has full mount; bottom player must escape using any technique — upa, elbow-knee escape, or heel drag.
topObjective: Maintain mount position, adjust to escape attempts, and attack when openings appear.
bottomObjective: Escape mount by any means — bridge and roll, shrimp to half guard, or trap and roll — and recover guard.
coachCue: Don't just bridge — bridge AND turn. Combine the upa with elbow-knee escape for a two-threat game.
scoring: 2pt per mount escape to guard, 3pt per escape to top, 1pt per remount, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player has side control with strong crossface; bottom player is working frames and hip escapes to recover guard.
topObjective: Maintain side control, advance to mount or north-south, and attack submissions from dominant position.
bottomObjective: Frame, hip escape, recover guard — use any technique to get back to a guard position.
coachCue: Get on your side first — flat on your back in side control means you're carrying all their weight.
scoring: 2pt per guard recovery, 2pt per mount or north-south transition, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player is standing in the bottom player's open guard; basic passing concepts — posture, grip fighting, footwork.
topObjective: Use correct passing mechanics — control the legs, manage distance, and complete a basic guard pass.
bottomObjective: Maintain guard using frames, hip movement, and foot placement to prevent the pass.
coachCue: Control both legs or no legs — having one leg controlled and one free is the most dangerous position for the passer.
scoring: 3pt per guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per guard recovery

situation: Bottom player has basic half guard with a knee shield; top player is working to flatten and pass.
topObjective: Work to kill the knee shield, establish an underhook, and begin passing the half guard.
bottomObjective: Maintain the knee shield, fight for the underhook, and look to recover full guard or set up a basic sweep.
coachCue: Bottom: your knee shield is your door — keep it between you and them at all times until you're ready to attack.
scoring: 2pt per sweep, 3pt per pass, 1pt per full guard recovery

situation: Film study round — recreating common ADCC positional patterns including leg lock exchanges, body lock passing, and wrestling-up sequences.
topObjective: Apply the recurring tactical patterns seen in ADCC — body lock passing, outside heel hooks from passing, and wrestling-up from bottom.
bottomObjective: Counter the ADCC meta-game strategies and apply your own competition game plan.
coachCue: In ADCC, the first player to stand and wrestle after a stalled guard exchange usually wins the scramble.
scoring: ADCC scoring: 3pt pass, 2pt sweep, 2pt takedown, 4pt mount/back, sub wins

situation: Multiple players on the mat; king-of-the-mat format where the winner stays and a new challenger enters.
topObjective: Win the exchange by sweep, pass, or submission and remain the king of the mat.
bottomObjective: Dethrone the current king by scoring a sweep, pass, or submission within the time limit.
coachCue: Stay composed — the king tires, so use your fresh energy wisely and attack immediately.
scoring: First to score wins: 1pt sweep, 1pt pass, instant win on submission

situation: Bottom player has closed guard; basic sweeping exchanges at live pace for beginners.
topObjective: Maintain posture and base inside the closed guard; resist sweep attempts and try to open the guard.
bottomObjective: Break posture and execute basic sweeps — scissor sweep, hip bump, or flower sweep — to get on top.
coachCue: Grip and then move your hips — the sweep comes from your legs and hips, not from pulling with your arms.
scoring: 2pt per sweep to top, 1pt per guard opening, 3pt per submission

situation: Attacker has back control with seatbelt grip and both hooks; working to finish the rear naked choke with various grips and setups.
topObjective: Finish the rear naked choke using short choke, palm-to-palm grip, or gable grip variations while maintaining back control.
bottomObjective: Defend the choke by fighting the hands, tucking the chin, and working to escape back control.
coachCue: Get your choking arm under the chin — don't squeeze until the blade of your forearm is across the throat.
scoring: 3pt per RNC finish, 2pt per choke locked (chin strap secured under chin), 1pt per escape from back

situation: Attacker has back control with seatbelt; defender is actively trying to escape by clearing hooks and turning.
topObjective: Maintain back control against active escape attempts — re-hook, adjust seatbelt, and transition between body triangle and hooks as needed.
bottomObjective: Escape back control by systematically clearing hooks and turning into the attacker.
coachCue: When they clear a hook, immediately switch to body triangle or re-hook with the other leg — never let both hooks go.
scoring: 1pt per 30 seconds of back control maintained, 2pt per full escape, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player has established side control; focus on attacking with americana, kimura, and arm-in chokes from the position.
topObjective: Attack submissions from side control — americana, kimura, arm-in guillotine, or north-south choke transitions.
bottomObjective: Defend submissions by keeping elbows tight, framing correctly, and escaping when the top player opens space to attack.
coachCue: Make them choose: defend the choke or defend the arm lock — attack in pairs, never one at a time.
scoring: 3pt per submission, 2pt per submission attempt that forces a defensive scramble, 1pt per escape to guard

situation: Top player has side control; bottom player is working live escapes to recover any guard position.
topObjective: Hold side control, shut down escape attempts, and advance position or submit.
bottomObjective: Escape to any guard — half guard, full guard, or create a scramble to get back to a neutral position.
coachCue: Create two escape threats at once — frame toward mount to make them shift, then shrimp the other way for guard.
scoring: 2pt per guard recovery, 2pt per mount transition, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player is in the bottom player's half guard; working to execute advanced passes — knee slide, leg weave, or backstep.
topObjective: Execute an advanced half guard pass — knee slide with crossface, leg weave, or backstep to leg drag.
bottomObjective: Defend the pass using frames, underhook battle, and guard retention; counter with sweeps when available.
coachCue: Knee slide: your knee must go to the mat on the far side of their hip — if it stalls on their thigh, you're stuck.
scoring: 3pt per completed pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per full guard recovery

situation: Top player is standing vs seated or supine guard; using quick footwork and misdirection to pass rather than pressure.
topObjective: Pass the guard using speed and timing — torreando, leg drag, or X-pass — without engaging in a pressure battle.
bottomObjective: Track the passer with your feet and hips, maintain guard, and counter-attack when the passer over-commits to one side.
coachCue: Change direction — speed passing works when you fake one way and go the other, not when you just run around the legs.
scoring: 3pt per guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per successful guard retention after a near-pass

situation: Bottom player has open guard (butterfly, DLR, or seated); working sweeps in live rounds.
topObjective: Shut down the open guard sweeps by controlling distance, staying balanced, and initiating guard passes.
bottomObjective: Sweep from open guard using hook sweeps, technical stand-ups, or overhead sweeps to achieve top position.
coachCue: Load them onto your butterfly hook before you sweep — if they're not on the hook, you're just pushing air.
scoring: 2pt per sweep to top, 3pt per guard pass, 1pt per guard retention

situation: Bottom player has an open guard (butterfly, single-leg X, or K-guard); working advanced submissions and transitions from guard.
topObjective: Pass the advanced open guard without getting caught in submissions or sweeps.
bottomObjective: Attack with advanced guard techniques — triangle from guard, armbar, guillotine, or leg lock entries from open guard.
coachCue: Threaten the sweep first — when they base to defend the sweep, the submission opens up.
scoring: 3pt per submission, 2pt per sweep, 3pt per guard pass, 1pt per submission attempt

situation: Players are in a clinch or front headlock position; attacker is looking to lock up the guillotine choke.
topObjective: Secure the chin strap grip, pull guard or sprawl, and finish the guillotine with correct mechanics.
bottomObjective: Defend the guillotine by posturing, circling away from the choking arm, and clearing the neck to a safe position.
coachCue: Connect your elbow to your hip when you close the guillotine — the squeeze comes from shortening the distance, not from arm strength.
scoring: 3pt per guillotine finish, 2pt per guillotine locked in guard, 1pt per successful defense and pass

situation: Various positions where the armbar is available — mount, guard, side control; attacker is systematically hunting the arm.
topObjective: Identify armbar opportunities from multiple positions and execute with correct hip placement, leg control, and finishing mechanics.
bottomObjective: Defend the armbar by keeping elbows tight, clasping hands, stacking, and escaping before the hips elevate.
coachCue: Pinch your knees before you extend — loose knees let them pull the arm out every time.
scoring: 3pt per armbar finish, 2pt per armbar position locked with hips elevated, 1pt per escape

situation: Film study round — recreating key positions from a recent professional match and drilling the exchanges live.
topObjective: Apply the winning strategies from the analyzed match — replicate the passing sequences, positional advances, and finishes.
bottomObjective: Apply the defensive strategies or counters that could have changed the match outcome.
coachCue: Identify the moment the match shifted — what grip, angle, or transition gave one player the advantage, and replicate it.
scoring: Match-specific scoring based on the techniques being studied

situation: Solo movement round — each player performs BJJ-specific movements on their own to build coordination and stamina.
topObjective: Execute hip escapes, technical stand-ups, granby rolls, and sit-throughs with correct form at increasing speed.
bottomObjective: Same as top — this is a solo drill round where each player works independently on fundamental movements.
coachCue: Hip escapes: shoulder, hip, feet — in that order, every single rep. Don't shortcut the sequence.
scoring: No scoring — focus on quality reps and movement fluency

situation: Partner drill round — cooperative drilling with a partner to build timing, feel, and coordination on fundamental techniques.
topObjective: Provide appropriate resistance for your partner's technique while building your own defensive awareness.
bottomObjective: Execute techniques with correct form against progressive resistance from a cooperative partner.
coachCue: Start at 30% resistance and build to 70% — your partner should succeed most reps but have to earn it.
scoring: No scoring — focus on clean technique repetitions and progressive resistance

situation: Bottom player has closed guard with advanced triangle setups available — overhook triangle, rubber guard entries, or mounted triangle transitions.
topObjective: Defend advanced triangle setups by maintaining posture, fighting grips, and recognizing the setups before they develop.
bottomObjective: Execute advanced triangle variations — overhook triangle, opposite-side triangle, or mounted triangle transition.
coachCue: Use the overhook to load the shoulder — when their shoulder crosses your centerline, the triangle is already halfway locked.
scoring: 3pt per triangle finish, 2pt per triangle locked, 1pt per defense that leads to posture recovery

situation: Bottom player has closed guard and has locked up the omoplata position on one arm; working to sweep rather than submit.
topObjective: Defend the omoplata by posturing, rolling forward, or clearing the leg before the sweep is completed.
bottomObjective: Use the omoplata position to sweep the top player face-down and come on top in a dominant position.
coachCue: Sit up into the omoplata and control their far hip — the sweep finishes when you flatten them forward, not when you crank the shoulder.
scoring: 2pt per omoplata sweep to top, 3pt per omoplata submission, 1pt per roll escape to neutral

situation: Bottom player has closed guard locked; focus is on basic guard maintenance — keeping posture broken, controlling distance, and not getting opened.
topObjective: Work to open the closed guard using posture, knee in the tailbone, and leg prying without getting swept or submitted.
bottomObjective: Maintain closed guard by breaking posture, controlling a wrist, and keeping your ankles locked behind their back.
coachCue: Ankles crossed, not feet — and pull them into you with your heels, don't just squeeze with your knees.
scoring: 1pt per guard opening, 1pt per posture break maintained for 5 seconds, 2pt per sweep or pass

situation: Bottom player has seated open guard; using a collar drag motion (no-gi: collar tie snap) to off-balance and take the back or come on top.
topObjective: Maintain balance and posture against the collar drag snap-down; recover base and initiate a guard pass.
bottomObjective: Snap the opponent's head down using a collar tie, shoot behind them for a back take, or come up to a front headlock.
coachCue: Snap and circle — the collar drag only works if you move laterally after the snap, not if you pull them straight down.
scoring: 3pt per back take off the drag, 2pt per front headlock, 1pt per guard pass, 1pt per failed drag defended

situation: Top player has full mount; focus is on maintaining the position through various grips, transitions between low and high mount, and grapevine control.
topObjective: Maintain mount for the full round using grapevines, high mount transitions, and swimming inside the frames.
bottomObjective: Escape mount using upa, elbow-knee escape, heel drag, or any technique to recover guard.
coachCue: Top: when they bridge, post and ride — when they shrimp, switch to high mount and block the knee with your foot.
scoring: 1pt per 30 seconds of mount maintained, 2pt per mount escape, 3pt per submission

situation: Top player has established mount; hunting for chokes — arm triangle, ezekiel (no-gi variation), and head-arm chokes from mount.
topObjective: Set up and finish chokes from mount — arm triangle, mounted guillotine, or no-gi ezekiel — using correct pressure and angle.
bottomObjective: Defend chokes by keeping hands near your neck, creating frames, and escaping before the choke is locked.
coachCue: Drive their arm across with your head, then lock the arm triangle — your head is the fifth limb in mount chokes.
scoring: 3pt per choke finish, 2pt per locked choke position, 1pt per escape to half guard or better

situation: Various scramble positions — opponent is turtled, in open guard, or in a transition; attacker is looking to systematically enter back control.
topObjective: Use a systematic approach to take the back — chair sit, truck entry, or arm drag to back — from various positions.
bottomObjective: Deny the back take by protecting the seatbelt line, clearing hooks before they set, and turning into the attacker.
coachCue: Seatbelt is the gateway — get the seatbelt from any position and the back take becomes mechanical.
scoring: 3pt per full back take with hooks, 2pt per seatbelt with one hook, 1pt per back take denied and guard recovered

situation: Top player has side control and is transitioning to north-south; working to maintain control through the transition.
topObjective: Transition smoothly from side control to north-south while maintaining chest pressure, then attack north-south choke or kimura.
bottomObjective: Use the transition moment to escape — frame during the spin and recover guard before north-south is established.
coachCue: Keep your chest glued to theirs during the spin — any space during the transition and they'll sneak a knee in.
scoring: 2pt per clean north-south established, 3pt per submission from north-south, 2pt per escape during transition

situation: Top player has knee on belly with one knee driving into the bottom player's abdomen; working submissions and transitions.
topObjective: Maintain knee on belly, force a reaction, and attack — far armbar, baseball choke (no-gi variation), or transition to mount.
bottomObjective: Frame against the knee, turn toward the top player, and escape to half guard, closed guard, or create a scramble.
coachCue: Drive the knee across their belly button, not on the ribs — grab the far wrist and wait for them to push your knee.
scoring: 1pt per 10 seconds of knee on belly, 3pt per submission, 2pt per escape to guard

situation: Both players are in an open leg entanglement (50/50 or outside ashi); focus is on understanding reaping mechanics and knee line control.
topObjective: Apply reaping pressure by controlling the knee line and turning the opponent's knee inward to expose the heel for attack.
bottomObjective: Defend the reap by tracking the knee line, keeping your knee aligned with your hip, and working to clear the entanglement.
coachCue: Control the knee line by pinching your knees together and curling your heels — your whole body creates the reap, not just your legs.
scoring: 2pt per heel exposure through reaping, 1pt per knee line cleared, 3pt per submission

situation: Attacker has a calf slicer or bicep slicer position locked; working to understand and apply compression lock mechanics.
topObjective: Apply the compression lock by correct wedge placement, driving the fulcrum into the muscle, and using body movement to finish.
bottomObjective: Defend the compression lock by straightening the limb, clearing the wedge, and escaping the entanglement.
coachCue: The calf slicer finishes when you triangle your legs and arch your back — the wedge does the work, not your arms.
scoring: 3pt per compression lock finish, 2pt per locked compression position, 1pt per escape

situation: Attacker has one leg entangled and is working to transition to the other leg when the defender clears the first attack.
topObjective: Flow between leg entanglements — inside sankaku to outside ashi, 50/50 to inside sankaku, or ashi to crab ride — to maintain offensive pressure.
bottomObjective: Defend each transition, clear your legs, and disengage or reverse to a dominant position.
coachCue: When transitioning legs, always control the new ankle before releasing the old position — no dead space between attacks.
scoring: 1pt per successful leg transition, 3pt per submission, 2pt per complete escape to neutral

situation: Top player is standing in the bottom player's open guard; focus is on managing the distance — too close invites sweeps, too far invites re-guard.
topObjective: Find and maintain the ideal passing distance — close enough to control the legs but far enough to avoid sweeps and submissions.
bottomObjective: Manipulate the distance to either pull the passer into sweep range or push them away to re-establish guard.
coachCue: Control the distance with your hands on their knees or ankles — if your hands are on the mat, you're too close.
scoring: 2pt per guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per distance reset to neutral

situation: Top player is facing a dynamic open guard (De La Riva, reverse De La Riva, or single-leg X) and must pass without getting swept.
topObjective: Shut down the open guard hooks, strip the grips, and execute a pass — torreando, leg drag, or long step — against an active guard player.
bottomObjective: Use open guard hooks to off-balance, sweep, or submit the passer when they commit to a passing lane.
coachCue: Strip the dangerous hook first — in DLR it's the hook on the front leg, in SLX it's the hook behind the knee.
scoring: 3pt per guard pass, 2pt per sweep, 1pt per guard retention after a near-pass

situation: Bottom player has butterfly guard with double underhooks or collar ties; actively working to elevate and sweep.
topObjective: Neutralize the butterfly hooks by sprawling, controlling the hips, and working to smash or go around the butterfly guard.
bottomObjective: Use butterfly hooks to elevate, off-balance, and sweep the top player; chain into submissions when the sweep is defended.
coachCue: Head in the center of their chest, double underhooks tight — when you elevate, kick the hook and turn the corner.
scoring: 2pt per butterfly sweep, 3pt per guard pass, 2pt per submission from butterfly

situation: Bottom player is in seated guard with feet on the floor, not yet engaging hooks; top player is standing and looking to engage.
topObjective: Engage the seated guard player with controlled grips and pass without getting pulled into a sweep or guillotine.
bottomObjective: Use the seated position to establish butterfly hooks, collar ties, or arm drags, and transition to a more active guard.
coachCue: Sitting guard: never sit flat — always have one hip up and one hand posted so you can move in any direction.
scoring: 2pt per sweep from seated guard, 3pt per guard pass, 1pt per guard transition (seated to butterfly, etc.)

situation: Passer has nearly completed a guard pass; bottom player must recover guard from a bad position — near side control, near mount, or scramble.
topObjective: Complete the guard pass and establish a dominant position; don't let the bottom player re-guard.
bottomObjective: Recover guard from a nearly-passed position using hip movement, frames, and knee reinsertions.
coachCue: Get your inside knee back between you and them — one knee inside is the difference between passed and guard.
scoring: 2pt per guard recovery, 3pt per completed pass to side control, 1pt per scramble to neutral

situation: Both players start standing in a competition stance; focus is on reactive takedowns — shooting off the opponent's movement or failed attack.
topObjective: Time your shot off the opponent's step, reach, or level change — execute a reactive single leg or double leg takedown.
bottomObjective: Defend the reactive shot with a sprawl, whizzer, or counter-shot and work to score your own takedown.
coachCue: Shoot when they step — a reactive shot into their forward momentum is twice as powerful as a standing start.
scoring: 2pt per takedown to top, 1pt per sprawl defense that leads to a front headlock, 3pt per submission

situation: Both players start standing in a clinch; working trips, foot sweeps, and upper body throws without gi grips.
topObjective: Execute trips (inside trip, outside trip) or throws (body lock throw, lateral drop) from the no-gi clinch.
bottomObjective: Defend the trip or throw by maintaining balance, countering with your own trip, or pulling guard safely.
coachCue: Inside trip: head on the same side as the tripping leg, drive your chest into theirs, and sweep the leg as you push forward.
duration: 3-min rounds, standing only, reset after each takedown
scoring: 2pt per trip or throw to ground, 1pt per sweep or off-balance, 3pt per throw to dominant position

situation: Attacker is seated with opponent in front; attacker pulls a front headlock and sits to guard to lock up a seated guillotine.
topObjective: Finish the seated or guard guillotine by locking the chin strap, closing the guard, and arching the back.
bottomObjective: Defend by posturing, circling to the weak side, and working to pass or clear the head before the choke locks.
coachCue: Seat belt your hands and arch — in the seated guillotine your back arch is the engine, not your arms.
scoring: 3pt per guillotine finish, 2pt per locked guillotine in guard, 1pt per defense to pass or neutral

situation: Top player is passing the guard and the bottom player's arm is trapped across their body, exposing the darce choke as the pass completes.
topObjective: Recognize the darce opening during the guard pass, lock the figure-four grip, and finish the darce choke while completing or abandoning the pass.
bottomObjective: Keep your elbows tight during the guard pass to deny the darce; if caught, fight the grip and get your arm back to center.
coachCue: Lock the darce as you pass — when their far underhook is deep during the pass, that arm is already halfway to being darced.
scoring: 3pt per darce finish, 2pt per darce locked during pass, 3pt per guard pass without darce, 1pt per escape

situation: Bottom player has a triangle locked from guard; the arm is trapped and the attacker is transitioning to an armbar without releasing the triangle.
topObjective: Transition from triangle to armbar by controlling the trapped arm, opening the legs, pivoting the hips, and extending the arm.
bottomObjective: Defend the triangle-to-armbar transition by pulling the arm free, posturing, or stacking before the armbar is locked.
coachCue: Keep the arm controlled with your legs as you pivot — if the arm escapes during the transition, you lose both submissions.
scoring: 3pt per armbar finish from triangle, 2pt per locked armbar position, 1pt per escape from the transition

situation: Attacker has back control with seatbelt and hooks; transitioning from RNC defense to an armbar by trapping the arm and rotating.
topObjective: Trap the choking arm, pivot your hips out, and finish the armbar from the back control position.
bottomObjective: Defend the armbar by pulling the arm back, tucking the elbow, and turning into the attacker to recover back control.
coachCue: Trap the wrist, plant your foot on their hip, and rotate your body perpendicular — the armbar from back is all about hip angle.
scoring: 3pt per armbar from back, 2pt per armbar attempt with hip rotation, 1pt per arm extraction and back recovery

situation: Competition-pace round — one player is pulling guard, the other is immediately working to pass; full resistance exchange.
topObjective: Pass the guard using any technique and advance to a scoring position under competition pressure.
bottomObjective: Sweep, submit, or maintain guard from the bottom under competition pressure; wrestle up if the guard is being shut down.
coachCue: The bottom player who wrestles up when their guard is being shut down wins more matches than the one who fights from their back.
scoring: ADCC scoring: 3pt pass, 2pt sweep, 2pt takedown, 4pt mount/back, sub wins

situation: Both players are in an open guard exchange; focus is on controlling the distance — when to be close, when to be far, and how to transition between the two.
topObjective: Manage distance by controlling grips, posting on hips, and choosing when to close distance for a pass or create space to disengage.
bottomObjective: Manipulate distance by pulling the opponent in for attacks or pushing away to reset and rebuild guard.
coachCue: The player who controls the distance controls the match — decide if you want to be close or far, never be in between.
scoring: 1pt per clean distance change (close to far or far to close), 2pt per sweep or pass, 3pt per submission

situation: Solo and partner hip movement drill round — shrimping, hip switches, granby rolls, and sit-outs with progressive resistance.
topObjective: Execute all hip movement patterns — hip escape, reverse hip escape, granby roll, hip switch, and sit-out — with smooth, continuous motion.
bottomObjective: Same as top — all players are drilling hip movements independently, then with a partner providing light resistance.
coachCue: Your hips are your engine — every escape, every sweep, every guard retention starts with your hips moving first.
duration: 5-min continuous, alternating solo and partner drills every 60 seconds
scoring: No scoring — focus on fluidity and hip movement quality
